author: @kendalcole7440
text:
I believe there is a great need for an inexpensive DIY alternative to commercial domestic battery banks for storage of self-generated off-grid electricity.

Given the advances in our understanding of the materials involved, and the ready availability of much of the information needed to make such a domestic storage solution (in terms of generation and management) one of the bottle necks is the cost associated with commercial batteries

Would you be interested in making a DIY video or project based on making an equivalent (by performance, not size) of a single 12v 60Ah rechargable battery for use in a domestic battery bank (specifically for use with some domestic generation such as PV, wind, peddle power, etc)?

There seems to be a gap in available info or instruction on this subject, and I believe a crowd-funding campaign &/or a book publication would easily provide any required material support.

Please consider doing this - many thanks.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Kendal Cole no worries mate - i'll try and get round to it

replies.author: @kendalcole7440
replies.text: If you do - I would like to support your efforts in what ways I can, for example, with some documentation (how-to's) for your website, or, to help with the editing or produce diagrams/graphics for a book on the subject if you're interested in producing one - there is a real need for this sort of info/instruction. Thanks.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Kendal Cole That's very kind mate - i have quite a lot to do and it is going to be sometime before i can get round to a book though it is something i want to do and what i can free up my schedule a bit i will start on it and send the manuscript to you if you like - though like i say - it will be some time

replies.text:
Ashley Mills Hi Ashley " the bearded one" Yes, you have the voice award of the 3 amigos Ha!


Ashley, I noticed Robert was behind the times on testing the super capacitor, could you give him a hand on setting up a multi meter with a USB cable to the computer for logging of power draw, you can check out my channel where I use the meter attached to my computer for long term testing of my DIY batteries, the system I use comes from Radio shack, pre packaged ready to go 


Good luck guys  and happy trails

author: @ScottSextonandDavidLewis
text:
I'm speechless, but the new microphone is working quite well.
I could invite you guys to sing again under a rather large lonesome pine in California, for our mountain cabin at 2 kilometers in elevation sits beneath a 2000 year old giant sequoia tree 100 meters in height and 5 meters in width. In person, It looks rather like a rocket to the stars because it is so wide and tall and singular, particularly when you see it under a full moon.
